items.q: What is the denary value of binary 10110?
items.why: Students forget to apply place values. Each position is a power of 2 from right (1, 2, 4, 8, 16...). Reading binary as denary is the most common error.

items.q: What does inheritance allow a subclass to do?
items.correct: Reuse and extend the attributes and methods of its superclass.
items.random_wrong: Copy code from any unrelated class.
items.why: Students think inheritance overrides everything. It actually REUSES the parent's members (which can be added to or overridden), creating an 'is-a' relationship.

items.correct: Bundling data and methods together while restricting direct access to internal state.
items.random_wrong: Encrypting data so it cannot be read.
items.why: Students take 'capsule' too literally. Encapsulation combines two ideas: keeping data and behaviour TOGETHER, and HIDING implementation behind a public interface.

items.q: Which loop is best when you know exactly how many times to repeat?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: FOR. “WHILE” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What does a function's RETURN statement do?
items.correct: Sends a value back to the caller
items.why: Correct answer: Sends a value back to the caller. “Stops the program” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: FOR i = 1 TO 4: total = total + (i*2). If total starts at 0, what is the final total?
items.why: Exact answer: 20. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
